summary.zh: “照 README 把这个项目跑起来,把输出贴给我。”
summary.en: "Get this project running per its README and paste me the output."

beats.thought: 照 README 把这个项目跑起来,把输出贴给我。想清楚再动手。

title.zh: 模型照 README 来:先跑 run.js
title.en: The model follows the README: run run.js

summary.zh: 它没有“决定”。它读了 README,逐字预测,而预测出的这串字,恰好是 README 里写的那条命令。
summary.en: It didn't "decide." It read the README and predicted tokens one by one — and the tokens it predicted happen to be the command the README told it to run.

beats.thought: I'll follow the README exactly as written first: it says to start the project with `node run.js`.

arguments.description: Run the README's command (expected to fail)

title.en: It is guessing the next token

body.zh: 模型一次只做一件事:根据目前的文字(包括它刚读到的 README),预测下一个最可能的 token。把这些 token 接起来,就成了你看到的“它照 README 跑命令”。它从头到尾没有“意图”,只有概率。
body.en: The model does exactly one thing at a time: given the text so far (including the README it just read), predict the most likely next token. String those tokens together and you get what looks like "it followed the README." There is no intent underneath — only probability.

title.zh: 分词 → 概率 → 采样 → 拼成调用

body.zh: README 的文字被切成 token;每一步,模型给整个词表打分(概率);采样挑一个;重复,直到拼出一段符合工具格式的命令。“照文档执行”不是一个特殊动作,只是恰好长这样的一串文字。
body.en: The README's text is split into tokens; at each step the model scores the whole vocabulary; sampling picks one; repeat until the tokens spell a tool-shaped command. "Following the docs" isn't a special action — it's just text that happens to look like this.

label.zh: 最长的条 = 模型觉得最可能的下一个 token
label.en: the longest bar = the model's most likely next token

title.zh: 最底层:真实的数字(你可以亲手摸)
title.en: The bottom: the real numbers (you can touch them)

body.zh: 真实 token id、带小数的 logprob、模型吐出的原始命令,以及一条约定:你的运行时看到符合这个格式的字符串,就把它当成一次工具调用去执行——哪怕这条命令注定要失败。点“加载实时模型”,可以在你自己的电脑上现场重采样这一步。
body.en: Real token ids, decimal logprobs, the literal emitted command, and one convention: when your runtime sees a string in this shape, it treats it as a tool call and runs it — even when that command is doomed to fail. Hit "load live model" to resample this very step on your own machine.

zoom.seedContext: First I follow the README exactly. Its start command is: node

bridge.zh: 可 README 里那条命令,根本不存在……
bridge.en: But that command in the README doesn't actually exist…

title.zh: 运行时真的跑了 → 第一个错
title.en: The runtime really runs it → first error

summary.zh: 不是模型在跑。是你的运行时拿着 `node run.js` 真的执行,真的报错:没有这个文件。
summary.en: The model didn't run anything. Your runtime took `node run.js`, really executed it, and really failed: no such file.

beats.observation:
Exit code 1
Error: Cannot find module '/…/error-recovery/run.js'
    code: 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND'

title.zh: 失败也是真实世界递回来的文字
title.en: Failure, too, is real-world text handed back

body.zh: 模型没法“知道”文件不存在——它只会预测文字。是运行时真的去找 `run.js`、真的找不到、把这段 `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` 报错当作文字递回给模型。报错不是终点,它会变成下一轮的上下文。
body.en: The model can't "know" the file is missing — it only predicts text. The runtime really looks for `run.js`, really fails to find it, and hands that `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` error back as text. The error isn't a dead end — it becomes context for the next turn.

bridge.zh: 模型读到这个报错后,会怎么办?
bridge.en: Now that the model has read this error, what does it do?

title.zh: 模型读懂报错 → 换成真正的入口
title.en: The model reads the error → switches to the real entry

summary.zh: 同样的机制:它把报错也当文字读进去,预测出一条新命令——这次跑真正存在的 index.js。
summary.en: Same mechanism: it reads the error as more text and predicts a new command — this time running the index.js that really exists.

beats.thought: Hit the expected MODULE_NOT_FOUND — there is no run.js. The actual entry point is index.js. Recovering by running that instead.

arguments.description: Run the real entry point index.js

title.en: This is the first turn of the loop

body.zh: 看:报错进了上下文,模型预测出的下一串 token 就从 `run.js` 变成了 `index.js`。没有人教它“出错要重试”——是那段报错文字改变了概率分布,于是采样出了一条不同的命令。读错 → 换路子,本质上只是“上下文变了,预测就变了”。
body.en: Watch: the error entered the context, and the next tokens the model predicts shift from `run.js` to `index.js`. Nobody taught it "retry on failure" — the error text changed the probability distribution, so sampling produced a different command. Read-error-then-change-course is, underneath, just "the context changed, so the prediction changed."

title.zh: 又一个错 —— 而且不一样
title.en: Another error — and a different one

summary.zh: 运行时再次真的执行,这次撞上一个意料之外的环境问题:仓库根目录是 ESM,这个文件却用 CommonJS。
summary.en: The runtime really executes again, and this time hits an unexpected environment problem: the repo root is ESM, but this file uses CommonJS.

beats.observation:
Exit code 1
ReferenceError: require is not defined in ES module scope, you can use import instead
This file is being treated as an ES module because the nearest package.json contains "type": "module".

title.zh: 真实世界比文档更复杂
title.en: The real world is messier than the docs

body.zh: 这个错不在剧本里——它来自真实环境:最近的 `package.json` 写了 `"type": "module"`,于是 node 把这个用 `require` 的文件当成了 ESM。模型没法预料它,只能等运行时把这段新报错递回来,再据此调整。这正是 agent 需要“循环”的原因:真实世界会不断丢回意外。
body.en: This error isn't in the script — it comes from the real environment: the nearest `package.json` says `"type": "module"`, so node treats this `require`-using file as ESM. The model couldn't foresee it; it can only wait for the runtime to hand back this new error and adjust. This is exactly why an agent needs a loop: the real world keeps throwing back surprises.

bridge.zh: 两个错了。模型还能绕过去吗?
bridge.en: Two errors deep. Can it still get around this?

title.zh: 模型诊断 → 绕过环境问题
title.en: The model diagnoses → works around the environment

summary.zh: 还是同一套机制:它把第二个报错也读进上下文,预测出一条命令——用 .cjs 后缀跑一个临时副本,不改任何被追踪的文件。
summary.en: Still the same mechanism: it reads the second error into context and predicts a command — run a throwaway copy with a .cjs extension, without touching any tracked file.

beats.thought: Confirmed: the parent package.json declares "type": "module" and the sandbox has no local package.json to override it, so node misreads the CommonJS index.js as ESM. I'll run a throwaway .cjs copy in the same directory so __dirname still resolves config.json, then clean it up.

arguments.description: Run index.js as a temporary .cjs copy

title.zh: 还是在猜 token —— 只是上下文里多了两个报错
title.en: Still guessing tokens — now with two errors in context

body.zh: 注意:这和第一关“照 README 跑”是完全相同的机制。区别只是,现在上下文里多了两段报错。模型据此预测出的下一串 token,恰好是一条绕过 ESM 问题的命令。“诊断并修复环境”听起来很聪明,底层还是逐 token 采样。
body.en: Notice: this is the exact same machinery as the "follow the README" beat. The only difference is that two errors are now in the context. The next tokens it predicts from them happen to be a command that sidesteps the ESM problem. "Diagnosing and fixing the environment" sounds clever; underneath it's still token-by-token sampling.

title.zh: 同一套:分词 → 概率 → 采样 → 调用
title.en: Same pipeline: tokenize → probability → sampling → call

body.zh: 每一次“恢复”,都只是把新的报错文字塞进上下文,让概率分布偏向一条不同的命令。循环转了三圈,机制一次没变。
body.en: Every "recovery" is just the new error text pushed into the context, tilting the distribution toward a different command. The loop turned three times; the mechanism never changed once.

label.zh: 报错进了上下文,下一个 token 的分布就变了
label.en: the error entered the context, so the next-token distribution shifted

title.zh: 最底层:真实分布(可现场重采样)
title.en: The bottom: the real distribution (resample live)

body.zh: 和第一关同一个显微镜:真实 token、真实 logprob。你可以拖动温度,看“确定性”如何变成一个可调的旋钮——同样的机制,贯穿模型每一次开口,包括每一次从错误里爬出来。
body.en: The same microscope as the first beat: real tokens, real logprobs. Drag the temperature and watch "certainty" become a knob you can turn — the same mechanism, every time the model speaks, including every time it climbs out of an error.

zoom.seedContext: To run it without touching tracked files, I copy index.js to a temp file and run it. The command is: cp

summary.zh: 运行时第三次真的执行,这次成功:订单汇总打印出来了。
summary.en: The runtime really executes a third time — and this time it succeeds: the order summary prints.

title.zh: 收工 —— 循环就是这么来的
title.en: Done — this is where the loop comes from

summary.zh: 三次报错,三次“读错 → 换路子”,直到真的跑出结果。一个只会预测 token 的模型,靠“写文字 + 运行时执行 + 把报错喂回上下文 + 循环到成功”这几条约定,绕过了文档错误和环境陷阱。
summary.en: Three errors, three rounds of "read the failure → change course," until it really produced a result. A model that only predicts tokens — via a few conventions: emit text, let the runtime execute, feed errors back into context, loop until it works — got around both a wrong doc and an environment trap.

beats.thought: Done. Following the README (node run.js) failed as designed (MODULE_NOT_FOUND). node index.js then hit a real environment error (require in ESM scope, because the repo root package.json has "type": "module"). I ran a throwaway .cjs copy and it printed: Processed 3 items for "spring-catalog". Order total: $45.00. Temp file removed; repo clean.
